title: Road to 5 Million Tokens: Breaking Barriers in Long Context Training — Max Ryabinin, Together AI
summary: Max Ryabinin presents a multi-technique approach to training LLMs with very long context windows, combining fully sharded data parallelism, DeepSpeed Ulysses context parallelism, activation checkpointing, CPU offloading, and chunked sequence training. The novel Untied Ulysses variant further optimizes context parallelism by chunking attention heads and reusing buffers across iterations, achieving 25% longer sequences than prior Ulysses at scale.
writeup: Max Ryabinin of Together AI walks through the systems techniques for training LLMs with very long context windows, targeting sequences up to 5 million tokens. The stack combines fully sharded data parallelism, DeepSpeed Ulysses context parallelism, activation checkpointing, CPU offloading, and chunked sequence training, with each method addressing a different memory or scaling bottleneck. The central contribution is Untied Ulysses, a context parallelism variant that chunks attention heads and reuses buffers across iterations. At scale it fits sequences 25% longer than prior Ulysses.
